title: microcerasus humilis
intro: Microcerasus humilis, also known as the dwarf hawthorn or red hawthorn, is a small shrub species native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its small, edible fruits and thorny branches. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and gastritis, due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Microcerasus humilis include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve circulation, and act as an antioxidant, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies. The plant can be prepared in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support the digestive system and promote overall well-being.
